name: Hover Telemark
preceding_figure_names: Basic Weave, Bounce Fallaway with Weave Ending, Closed Impetus and Feather Finish, Fallaway Reverse and Slip Pivot, Feather Step, Hover Feather, Natural Hover Telemark, Natural Telemark, Natural Twist Turn, Natural Weave, Natural Zig-Zag from PP, Open Telemark and Feather Ending, Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending, Reverse Turn, Top Spin, Weave from PP
following_figure_names: Feather Step (step 1 in CBMP OP), Basic Weave (LF fwd in line with partner on toe into overturned Basic Weave), Hover Feather (steps 1-2 counted SS can be followed by Hover Feather), Natural Turn (at corner), Natural Weave (at corner), Natural Telemark (at corner), Open Telemark, Natural Turn, Outside Swivel and Feather Ending (at corner), Natural Twist Turn (at corner), Natural Zig-Zag from PP (at corner), Natural Hover Telemark (at corner), Curved Feather to Back Feather (at corner)
notes: Hover Telemark can finish in Promenade Position. See Hover Telemark to PP.

steps.action: LF forward, start turning left, compress right shoulder, use CBM sending lady inside turn, stretch abdominal muscle through strong diagonal to the left
comments: Compress right shoulder (hip and foot), use CBM sending Lady to inside of turn, stretch abdominal muscle through strong diagonal to the left

steps.action: RF to side, LF brushes towards RF, facing LOD, T (RF) then IE of T of LF, sway left, continue rise
LF to side and slightly forward, pointing DC, body turns less, sway, up, lower
comments: When landing on LF, small range of motion to recover from sway.

steps.action: RF forward in CBMP, OP, facing DC, sway, CBM
RF back, backing DW, start turning left, sway, CBM, start rise, NFR
comments: Small CBM through the hip allowing L hip to release back. Step RF back, anchor right shoulder. R hip is moving across inside of right foot which will allow the hip to swing. Body is picked up through strong diagonal.

steps.action: LF to side, RF brushes towards LF, backing LOD, T (LF) then IE of T of RF, sway right, continue rise
comments: When traveling backwards, never turn shoulders so you don't cross center line. When going back, upper body is not a rotary motion, body should be stretching diagonal.
Left side is very lengthened, stretched. Head shaped to right. Keep elbows in front of body. Pick up stomach back to spine.

steps.action: RF to side and slightly back, backing DC, body turns less, sway, up, lower, dissolve sway
LF back in CBMP, backing DC, sway, CBM
